claim: The text could encode a Malay or Southeast Asian language, based on word structure similarities to Austronesian languages with prefixing and suffixing morphology
evidence_against: Never developed into a full decipherment. Some structural parallels noted but insufficient for proof. Guy himself treated it as speculative.

tests_run.details: Never developed into a full decipherment. Some structural parallels noted but insufficient for proof. Guy himself treated it as speculative.

reasoning: Historical attempt by Jacques Guy (1995). Cipher type: phonetic encoding. Proposed language: Malay/Austronesian.
source: Guy's posts to the Voynich mailing list (1990s); Kennedy & Churchill (2004)
